text: Drafting attorney Jalen R. Harris admitted starting a brief on Westlaw then "resorting to ChatGPT," submitting nine hallucinated case citations without checking them. The court imposed $250 on Harris and $1,000 on supervising attorney James Roquemore under Rule 11, and formally admonished co-counsel Corwin St. Raymond. Harris took full responsibility and acknowledged he never read the fabricated cases.
deployer: Jalen R. Harris; James Roquemore; Corwin St. Raymond
canonical_source_conflicted: Nine non-existent federal cases cited in the brief; genuine federal law
mitigation_gap: Drafting attorney used ChatGPT without verifying output; supervising attorney failed to catch the hallucinations.
reliance_or_harm: $1,250 in monetary sanctions ($250 + $1,000); formal admonishment of co-counsel.
